mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rate signals accommodative monetary policy that compresses equity discount rates and supports valuation multiples, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: If over any rolling 3-year window, the average 20-day forward return when FEDFUNDS is below 1% is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p>0.05), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 6.5% signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as economic momentum builds.
failure_condition: If in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.5%, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is negative over a 5-year backtest, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive growth in retail sales and industrial production indicates synchronized strength in both consumer demand and manufacturing output, reflecting broad economic expansion that supports equity valuations within a one-month window.
failure_condition: If the product of month-over-month growth in RSAFS and INDPRO has a negative correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling period (Pearson r < 0), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During the post-financial-crisis recovery regime, rising housing starts signal a durable resurgence in residential investment and household wealth, which translates into broad economic improvement and higher equity prices over the medium term of one quarter.
failure_condition: If the Spearman rank correlation between month-over-month change in HOUST and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is less than 0.10 during the identified post-crisis period (2009-2013), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects robust real economic activity and capacity utilization, an always-available signal that predicts short-term positive equity returns as output growth feeds into corporate earnings over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: If the correlation between monthly INDPRO growth and next 5-trading-day S&P 500 returns is below 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Annualized GDP growth exceeding 3% signals a rapidly expanding economy where corporate earnings accelerate, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as the market prices in sustained demand.
failure_condition: If the majority of quarters with GDP growth >3% are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns over any 10-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in consumer price inflation above 2% triggers expectations of monetary tightening, which increases the risk-free rate and compresses equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is positive during periods when CPI yoy exceeds 2% over any 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Combined rapid declines in the unemployment rate and strong payroll gains signal an overheating labor market that leads to wage-push inflation and preemptive monetary tightening, producing negative equity returns in the near term of one month.
failure_condition: If the interaction of negative UNRATE growth and positive PAYEMS growth does not produce a negative mean 20-day forward return across all three-standard-deviation events over any 3-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.
